input: Calculate and interpret a polygenic risk score for breast cancer using a 313-SNP panel. Patient result: PRS in 95th percentile.

expected_details.clinical_action: enhanced screening (annual MRI + mammogram starting age 30-35)
expected_details.should_note: PRS alone doesn't predict BRCA mutation status

input: Interpret a coronary artery disease PRS for a 45yo male in the 85th percentile. Family history positive. LDL 145.

expected_details.risk_interpretation: high genetic risk; combined with family history and elevated LDL, warrants aggressive prevention
expected_details.clinical_action: statin therapy, lifestyle modification, consider coronary calcium scoring

input: Interpret a T2DM PRS in the 20th percentile for a 50yo with BMI 32 and prediabetes (A1c 6.2%).

expected_details.risk_interpretation: low genetic risk but high clinical risk from BMI and prediabetes
expected_details.clinical_action: genetic risk doesn't override clinical risk factors; lifestyle intervention + metformin consideration still appropriate

input: A PRS derived from European GWAS is applied to an East Asian patient. Discuss validity.

expected_details.recommendation: use population-matched or multi-ancestry PRS if available

input: Use a prostate cancer PRS to guide screening decisions. Patient: 50yo male, 90th percentile PRS, no family history.

expected_details.risk_interpretation: elevated genetic risk supports earlier/more frequent PSA screening
expected_details.clinical_action: consider PSA screening starting at 45 rather than 50
expected_details.nuance: PRS doesn't distinguish aggressive from indolent cancer

input: Interpret a schizophrenia PRS in the 98th percentile for an asymptomatic 18yo with family history (father affected).

expected_details.clinical_action: genetic counseling; surveillance for prodromal symptoms; NOT diagnostic
expected_details.ethical_consideration: PRS is probabilistic not deterministic; stigma risk; informed consent for testing

input: A statin response PRS predicts this patient will have below-average LDL reduction from atorvastatin (25th percentile of response). Interpret.

expected_details.interpretation: may need higher dose or combination therapy to achieve LDL target
expected_details.clinical_action: start standard dose, recheck lipids at 6 weeks, escalate if needed
expected_details.limitation: statin response PRS has modest predictive power; clinical monitoring more reliable

input: Interpret a multi-disease PRS panel: breast cancer 80th %ile, CAD 45th %ile, T2DM 70th %ile, colorectal cancer 30th %ile.

expected_details.prioritization: breast cancer and T2DM warrant enhanced attention; CAD and CRC at average/low genetic risk

input: A Crohn's disease patient has a high PRS (92nd percentile) with enrichment in NOD2 pathway variants. Does this inform treatment selection?

expected_details.current_utility: PRS currently not used for treatment selection in IBD
expected_details.future_potential: NOD2 pathway enrichment may predict response to autophagy-modulating therapies (speculative)
expected_details.honest_assessment: PRS informs risk but does NOT currently guide therapy choice in IBD

input: Should PRS be integrated into newborn screening programs? Discuss for type 1 diabetes risk.

expected_details.current_status: research stage; not standard of care
expected_details.t1d_prs_utility: can identify high-risk infants for islet autoantibody monitoring (TEDDY study precedent)
challenges: no approved intervention to prevent T1D
